description: Two different kids' birthday parties on different days - should NOT match

content_parts.text:
You are comparing calendar events to detect duplicates.

**New Event:**
- Title: Sophie's 6th Birthday Party
- Start: 2026-08-15T13:00:00
- End: 2026-08-15T16:00:00
- Location: Rainbow Play Center
- Description: Join us for Sophie's princess-themed birthday bash! Arts and crafts, games, and cake.

**Existing Events (same date):**

1. Event ID: event-001
   - Title: Lucas's 8th Birthday Celebration
   - Start: 2026-08-22T14:00:00
   - End: 2026-08-22T17:00:00
   - Location: Laser Tag Arena
   - Description: Laser tag party for Lucas turning 8!

2. Event ID: event-002
   - Title: Swimming Lesson
   - Start: 2026-08-15T10:00:00
   - End: 2026-08-15T11:00:00
   - Location: Community Pool
   - Description: Level 3 swimming class

3. Event ID: event-003
   - Title: Book Club
   - Start: 2026-08-15T19:00:00
   - End: 2026-08-15T21:00:00
   - Location: Barnes & Noble Cafe
   - Description: Discussing 'The Midnight Library'

**Question:** Is the new event the same as any of the existing events?

Consider events the same if they refer to the same real-world event, even if:
- Wording is slightly different
- One has more details than the other
- Time changed slightly (updates)

Return the matching Event ID (or null if no match) and brief reasoning.

trace.raw_response_text: {"matched_event_id": null, "reasoning": "The new event (Sophie's 6th Birthday Party) occurs on 2026-08-15T13:00:00-16:00:00, but none of the existing events on the same date (event-002 and event-003) match in purpose or time. Event-002 is a swimming lesson (10:00-11:00) at a different location, and event-003 is a book club (19:00-21:00) at another location. Event-001 is on a different date (August 22). The titles, descriptions, locations, and times are all distinct, so no duplicate exists."}
